summary: Design spec and implementation plan for lib.is_terminal_state(state) -> bool: a pure helper returning True for the three terminal engine states ('done', 'failed', 'blocked') and False for all other inputs including None and non-strings. Single task: TDD approach appending tests to tests/test_engine.py then inserting the function after PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS in lib.py.

ledger.what: The complete set of terminal states is exactly {"done", "failed", "blocked"}
ledger.why: Issue specifies these three; PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS in lib.py enumerates the same three non-setup special keys, corroborating the set
ledger.what_i_did: Verified against PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS at lib.py lines 714-718; 'setup' is the init key (not terminal); no other terminal states appear in the codebase

blast_radius.why: Pure predicate; a wrong set would cause silent mis-classification only in callers of this helper, none of which exist yet

reversibility.why: The constant set is a one-line change

ledger.revisit_if: A new terminal state is introduced (e.g. 'cancelled')

ledger.what: Placement of is_terminal_state in lib.py immediately after PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS / PHASE_LABEL_COLOR
ledger.why: Groups the terminal-state concept with the only other enumeration of these keys; avoids an arbitrary position

blast_radius.why: Placement is cosmetic and does not affect runtime behaviour

reversibility.why: Trivial to move the function definition to a different line

ledger.revisit_if: Reviewer prefers a different location in lib.py

ledger.what: Tests go in a new section of tests/test_engine.py rather than a new file
ledger.why: test_engine.py already covers protocol-agnostic lib surface; a new section keeps related unit tests co-located without proliferating small files
ledger.what_i_did: Appended a '# Section: is_terminal_state' block following the existing section convention

blast_radius.why: Test file location does not affect runtime or correctness

reversibility.why: Tests can be moved to a new file at any time

ledger.revisit_if: Future test file split policy changes or test_engine.py grows unwieldy

ledger.what: isinstance(state, str) is the correct non-string guard, handling None, ints, lists without raising
ledger.why: Python's isinstance returns False for None and all non-string types without raising; this is the standard defensive pattern
ledger.what_i_did: Verified: isinstance(None, str) is False; isinstance(42, str) is False — standard stdlib behaviour

blast_radius.why: Pure defensive check; wrong guard would cause TypeError for non-strings, which is easily caught by the tests

reversibility.why: Guard implementation is a trivial one-liner change

ledger.revisit_if: A subclass of str should be excluded (extremely unlikely in this codebase)
